design_philosophy: Industrial Enterprise Tool. Precise, structured, data-dense, technical aesthetic mirroring automotive manufacturing engineering culture. Avoids consumer-app fluff. Color is used strictly for function, status, and hierarchy.

dynamic_gauge_logic.rule: Gauge color logic dynamically changes based on the current month to reflect physical floor tags.

spacing_and_layout.grid_system: MODE B: THE CONTROL ROOM GRID
rules: Base Grid: grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 or 12-col dense grids.
Every pixel utilized, no jagged bottoms (h-full).
Padding: Containers with borders must have internal padding (p-4 to p-6).
Optical alignment: Use right padding for icons placed on the left side of buttons.

buttons.primary: bg-[#CC0000] text-white hover:bg-[#A30000] rounded-sm font-semibold text-sm px-4 py-2 transition-colors
buttons.secondary: bg-white text-gray-700 border border-gray-300 hover:bg-gray-50 rounded-sm font-semibold text-sm px-4 py-2 transition-colors
buttons.ghost: text-gray-600 hover:bg-gray-100 hover:text-gray-900 rounded-sm font-semibold text-sm px-4 py-2 transition-colors

tables.style: High-density data tables. Monospace fonts for all metric/numeric columns.
tables.header: bg-gray-50 text-gray-500 text-xs uppercase tracking-wider font-bold border-b border-gray-200 py-3 px-4

forms.inputs: rounded-sm border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:border-[#CC0000] focus:ring-1 focus:ring-[#CC0000] outline-none transition-all
forms.labels: block text-xs font-bold text-gray-700 uppercase tracking-wide mb-1

badges.base: inline-flex items-center px-2 py-0.5 rounded-sm text-xs font-bold font-mono uppercase border

navigation_sidebar.style: Dark mode sidebar contrasting with light main content area.
navigation_sidebar.container: bg-[#000000] border-r border-gray-800 text-gray-300 w-64 min-h-screen

motion.philosophy: Minimalist and functional. No distracting bounce or spring effects.
motion.hover_states: Subtle border darkening or background shifts. Explicit text colors on hover.

icons.usage: Use bold or regular weights for clarity in dense data environments. Avoid overly playful/rounded icons.

login_background.description: High-tech robots assembling a car in a modern factory setting. Use with a heavy dark overlay (e.g., bg-black/70) so the login card stands out.

empty_state_gauge.description: Close up of a pressure gauge on a machine. Good for 'No gauges found' or 'Calibration report empty' states.

requirements: All interactive elements MUST include data-testid attribute.
Format: kebab-case, role-based. e.g., data-testid='login-submit-button', data-testid='approve-gauge-workflow'.

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly adhere to the dynamic color logic for gauges: the month of the year dictates whether User Dept / Calibration Dept tags are Green or Yellow. Implement a utility function to compute this.

Use Shadcn UI for complex components (Tables, Dialogs, Selects) but heavily override their styles to remove the generic 'rounded-md / soft shadow' Vercel look. Force 'rounded-sm' and strict 1px borders.
The UI must employ the 'Control Room Grid' for dashboards. It should look like an industrial HMI (Human-Machine Interface), dense and exact.
For the Calibration reading table, use 'IBM Plex Mono' (tabular-nums) so decimal points align perfectly in the Error and Tolerance columns.
Do NOT use placeholder images. Rely on the provided Pexels/Unsplash URLs.
Never use fully transparent backgrounds behind text. Always ensure minimum 4.5:1 contrast.
